ground_truth_summary: Pac-Man themed pop-art trading card with a yin-yang symbol. LEFT side is YELLOW containing a RED GHOST (white eyes looking right). RIGHT side is RED containing a YELLOW PAC-MAN (mouth open to right). Background is CYAN with concentric BLACK CIRCLES (not spirals). Text: pacman 46 / 010 / ZEN MASTER / "Inner peace!" / RARE / 24,620 / beat my score → / pacman46.com.

notes: This is a tricky benchmark because the critical facts are: (1) yellow=left+ghost, red=right+pacman (the 'orientation trap' — many models say reversed), (2) background is CONCENTRIC CIRCLES not spiral, (3) background is CYAN.
Only ONE model (gemma4-e4b|think) failed catastrophically by inventing two Pac-Man characters and missing the ghost entirely. This is a true hallucination.

Most models correctly identified ghost-in-yellow and Pac-Man-in-red. The most common minor error was spatial framing as top/bottom halves (qwen3.5-4b|think, qwen3.6-27b|nothink, qwen3.6-27b|think, qwen3.6-35b-a3b|think) rather than left/right halves — but the character-to-color mapping was still correct.

Concentric circles vs spiral: No model explicitly claimed pure spiral; several used mixed phrasing ('concentric circles spiraling outward', 'concentric circles (a spiral)') which is acceptable paraphrasing. The Gemma-26b 'spiral' qualifier is borderline.
Cyan color correctly identified by all models (variously as teal, cyan, turquoise, light blue) — all acceptable.
Qwen3.5/3.6 family performed best overall: 6 of top 6 scores. Gemma4-31b was the standout Gemma.

think vs nothink: For qwen3.5-4b and qwen3.5-9b, the nothink variants slightly outperformed their think counterparts (cleaner spatial and pattern descriptions). For qwen3.6-27b and qwen3.6-35b-a3b, nothink and think scored similarly.

Deterministic scoring (from input) correlates well with holistic judgment — the only divergence is gemma4-e4b|think which had det_mean 9.0 but the response contains a major ghost-miss hallucination that the holistic score penalizes heavily.
